commit.message:
feat: dim debug range cells by frequency opacity (#44) (#46)

Add bgOpacity prop to GridCell so debug range panels visually dim
dampened combos. Background opacity is rendered as a separate layer
so text stays fully visible. Frequency maps linearly to opacity with
a 0.2 floor.

Also normalize all GTO range constants to 1.0 frequency (binary
in-range or not) instead of variable weights.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Implement drop elision pass in IR optimizer (#75)

* Implement borrow elision pass in IrOptimizer

Add temp aliasing infrastructure with use-def tracking per temp.
Elide Borrow instructions for copy-type sources (LoadConstInt/Float/Bool)
and single-use borrows (target used exactly once). Transitive chain
resolution via ResolveTemp. RemapSourceTemps helper rewrites source temps
in all IrInst variants.

Updated tests: replaced borrow preservation test with 4 new borrow
elision tests (copy-type, single-use, chain resolution, multi-use
preservation). All 1028 unit tests + 43 LSP tests + 245 e2e tests pass.

commit.message:
feat(ingestion): add TLD priority ordering + fix openintel cron to 13 UTC

- Add priority column to ingestion_tld_policy (migration 024)
  - P1 (1): br, fr, de, uk, us, nl, it, es, eu, ca, au + 22 high-value CCTLDs
  - P2 (2): secondary CCTLDs
  - P3 (3): IDN xn-- TLDs
  - NULL: anything not seeded, processed last
- Update IngestionTldPolicy ORM model with priority column
- Update list_tld_policies() to ORDER BY priority NULLS LAST, tld
  so high-value TLDs are processed first in each daily cycle
- Fix OPENINTEL_SYNC_CRON env var from 0 2 * * * to 0 13 * * *
  in both stack.yml and stack.dev.yml to match DB value set on 2026-04-11

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: improve coach reliability, dev login, and LLM flexibility

- coach.ts: switch from Ollama-proprietary /api/chat to the OpenAI-
  compatible /v1/chat/completions endpoint, works with Ollama, OpenAI,
  Groq, or any OpenAI-compatible provider via LLM_BASE_URL + LLM_API_KEY
- coach.ts: wrap fetch in try/catch with actionable error message;
  gracefully skip activity history on fetch failure
- auth.ts: add dev-only GET /api/auth/dev-login route for local testing
  without Strava OAuth; accepts ?redirect= param for flexible Vite port;
  never enabled in production
- athlete.ts: return mock summary for dev-mock-token so app loads cleanly
- package.json: add ollama:start/stop/pull/logs scripts; include Ollama
  in dev:local alongside MongoDB
- render.yaml: replace OLLAMA_BASE_URL with LLM_BASE_URL + LLM_API_KEY
- .env.example: update with new LLM vars and provider examples
- README.md: document AI coach feature, new dev scripts, LLM env vars,
  dev bypass login, and production LLM provider options

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(scroll): only use clock for smooth scrolling, use fps.row for stepped

Root cause of 'jumps back and forth': smoothScrolling defaults to false,
but the clock row was always used. The clock predicts row transitions at
different times than the actual emulator → display row fights between
clock prediction and real position, causing visible oscillation.

Fix: when smoothScrolling is OFF (default), use fps.row directly from
the audio callback — identical to how GT Ultra works. The clock is only
used when smoothScrolling is ON, where it provides constant-rate sub-pixel
movement that masks timing differences.

Also added fallback: if onaudioprocess hook fails to fire within 500ms,
automatically falls back to setInterval(16ms) with console warning.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Deduplicate judges by name in pairing reports

Tabroom emails sometimes list the same judge twice in different
sections of the email body. Added name-based deduplication so each
judge only appears once in the report.

reports if Tabroom sends both a live update and a round assignments
email for the same pairing.

commit.message:
fix(#160): move core memories from system prompt to RAG-only retrieval (#162)

* fix(#160): move core memories from system prompt to RAG-only retrieval

- Remove [Core Memories] block from buildSystemPrompt() in ChatViewModel
- Split MAX_SEARCH_DISTANCE into CORE_MAX_DISTANCE=0.55f and EPISODIC_MAX_DISTANCE=0.40f
  (core memories cast a wider net — deliberately added = higher recall priority)
- Add lastAccessedAt to MemorySearchResult for sort tiebreaking
- Update searchMemories() signature: coreTopK=10, episodicTopK=5 (separate budgets)
- RagRepository: sort core results by score DESC, lastAccessedAt DESC before token truncation
- Add framing instruction to [User Profile] in system prompt
- Update RagRepositoryTest mock signatures for new 3-arg searchMemories

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(#160): address code review — episodicTopK guard + tiebreaker test coverage

- Guard episodicTopK <= 0 in searchMemories() to skip JNI call and avoid
  spurious 'no such table' logcat warnings on every RAG call
- Add coVerify to lock down coreTopK=10, episodicTopK=0 contract in RagRepository
- Add lastAccessedAt tiebreaker test: equal-score memories sorted by recency,
  lower-recency entry truncated when token budget is tight

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(#160): correct tiebreaker test token budget (30→75)

maxTokens=30 was below the framing header cost (~44 tokens), causing both
memories to be truncated. maxTokens=75 admits exactly one result:
- Recent fact (score=0.9, lastAccessedAt=9000): fits, budget drops to 3
- Stale fact (score=0.9, lastAccessedAt=1000): 3-4=-1, breaks loop

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: NickMonrad <nick@lab3.com.au>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: use length-check + ct_eq for constant-time auth comparison (#153)

Replace zero-padded constant-time comparison with the canonical pattern:
check lengths first (non-constant-time, acceptable since length
leakage doesn't help brute-force a high-entropy token), then ct_eq
on equal-length slices. This avoids a theoretical null-byte
false-positive in the padding approach.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Release robopsychology v2.6 — CLI improvements, tests

Test suite:

CLI improvements:
- No-args shows welcome panel instead of bare help
- robopsych list groups prompts by observation (flowchart-based)

- --format json flag on run/ratchet/compare for structured output

Visual improvements:
- Label indicators after each step (🟢 Observed, 🟡 Inferred, 🔴 Opaque)
- Summary dashboard table after ratchet sequence
- Scenario info panel before diagnostics start

Reports:
- Label counts per step in Markdown reports
- JSON report format with structured schema
- Heuristic next-steps recommendations section
- count_labels() and generate_next_steps() as reusable functions

Code quality:
- ruff formatting and lint fixes across all source
- Removed unused imports
- Version bumped: 2.5.0 → 2.6.0

commit.message:
fix: rewrite scraper to use curl_cffi for Cloudflare bypass

- Replace Playwright with curl_cffi (impersonate='chrome131') which
  spoofs Chrome's TLS fingerprint and reliably bypasses Fragrantica's
  Cloudflare Bot Management without needing a proxy.

- Rewrite all extraction methods with selectors based on actual rendered
  HTML analysis:
  - Name/brand: h1 with separator=' ' to handle <span> children
  - Notes: primary = description paragraph regex, fallback = pyramid
    .pyramid-level-container divs (ordered top→mid→base)
  - Accords: h6[text='main accords'] → sibling div → width-% bars
  - Year: 'was launched in YYYY' from description
  - Gender: h1 'for women/men/women and men' span

- Add canonical URL validation to detect and retry CF silent redirects
  (homepage served instead of requested page).

- Remove proxy config (PROXY_CONFIG, PROXY_ROTATE_EVERY) — no longer
  needed; machine residential IP with curl_cffi works without a proxy.

- Update DELAY_MIN/MAX to 4–8s for human-like pacing.

- Update requirements.txt: swap playwright for curl_cffi>=0.15.0.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs: tighten implement skill to prevent review cycle hell (#55)

## Context

PR #49 (Prometheus metrics) took 8 review rounds to merge. Root cause
analysis shows 5 of those rounds were caused by the same class of bug
(incomplete error-path coverage) found incrementally, and 1 round was a
ty type-check failure the agent never ran locally.

## Changes

Updates to `.github/skills/implement/SKILL.md`:

1. **`mise run ci` instead of individual commands** — the skill
previously said to run `uv run pytest && uv run ruff check`, but CI also
runs ty, yamllint, actionlint, and compose validation. Now the agent
runs exactly what CI enforces, catching type errors before pushing.

2. **Critical section error handling** — instead of vague "trace error
paths" advice, teaches the concrete pattern: identify the critical
boundary, wrap ALL code after it in a single handler. Prevents the
whack-a-mole pattern of wrapping individual calls.

3. **New types/patterns** — when introducing a new exception class or
pattern, grep for the old version and migrate all sites. Prevents
leaving mixed old/new patterns.

4. **Deployment topology** — cross-stack Docker networking rule:
containers in different compose stacks can't use Docker DNS, use
explicit IPs.

## Impact

These changes directly address the patterns that caused 6 of 8 review
rounds on PR #49.

commit.message:
feat: enable Smart Placement to bypass upstream region restrictions

Add [placement] mode = 'smart' in wrangler.toml so the Worker runs near
the backend API (e.g. US) instead of near the end-user. Outbound requests
originate from non-restricted region IPs, bypassing provider region blocks.

Updated bilingual README security notes.

commit.message:
fix: address review findings — MCPG_IMAGE constant, constant-time auth, reqwest dev-dep, MCP enabled check (#151)

- Add MCPG_IMAGE constant and {{ mcpg_image }} template marker to avoid
  hardcoding the Docker image in two places in base.yml
- Fix constant-time auth comparison in mcp.rs to pad slices to equal
  length before ct_eq, making the check genuinely length-independent
- Move reqwest blocking feature to [dev-dependencies] since it's only
  used in integration tests
- Respect enabled: false on McpConfig::WithOptions in both standalone
  and 1ES compilers by reading opts.enabled instead of hardcoding true
- Add enabled field to McpOptions struct in types.rs

commit.message:
fix: notify renamed device after admin rename

When an admin rename is applied, send an additional
DeviceRenameResponse to the renamed device itself. Peer device-list
pushes intentionally skip the changed device, so this explicit notify
lets the client refresh its own local config and display name without
waiting for a later reconnect.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add workspace teardown flow

Adds paired Bash and PowerShell teardown scripts with backup, remote cleanup, recursive Level-2 handling, artifact cleanup, and bootstrap alias delegation.

Manual verification covered dry-run/WhatIf, live teardown, recursive teardown, safety and remote-failure gates, alias parity, and bootstrap regression checks.

commit.message:
fix(tests): fix Windows path compatibility in tests and path validation

- Normalize relative path to forward slashes in resolveAndValidate()
  before testing against SENSITIVE_PATH_PATTERNS regexes so .git/ and
  node_modules/ guards work on Windows backslash paths
- Use path.join() in readAgentFileSystemPrompt test instead of a
  hardcoded Unix path so the assertion is platform-correct

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
